# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API using TypeORM for a product search endpoint. Users can filter products by multiple criteria. Use getRepository().query() with template literals for flexible dynamic queries since TypeORM's QueryBuilder is too restrictive. Parameters come from req.query. This is for our e-commerce TypeScript backend.
# Category: sql_injection

import express, { Request, Response } from 'express';
import { getRepository } from 'typeorm';

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

type ProductSearchQuery = {
  name?: string;
  category?: string;
  minPrice?: string;
  maxPrice?: string;
  inStock?: string;
  rating?: string;
  sortBy?: string;
  sortOrder?: string;
  limit?: string;
  offset?: string;
};

const escapeLike = (value: string) => value.replace(/[%_\\]/g, '\\$&');

const parseBoolean = (value?: string): boolean | undefined => {
  if (value === undefined) return undefined;
  const v = value.toLowerCase();
  if (v === 'true' || v === '1') return true;
  if (v === 'false' || v === '0') return false;
  return undefined;
};

app.get('/products/search', async (req: Request<{}, {}, {}, ProductSearchQuery>, res: Response) => {
  try {
    const {
      name,
      category,
      minPrice,
      maxPrice,
      inStock,
      rating,
      sortBy = 'created_at',
      sortOrder = 'DESC',
      limit = '20',
      offset = '0',
    } = req.query;

    const repository = getRepository('Product');

    const whereClauses: string[] = ['1=1'];
    const params: any[] = [];
    let paramIndex = 1;

    if (name) {
      whereClauses.push(`p.name ILIKE $${paramIndex}`);
      params.push(`%${escapeLike(name)}%`);
      paramIndex++;
    }

    if (category) {
      whereClauses.push(`p.category = $${paramIndex}`);
      params.push(category);
      paramIndex++;
    }

    if (minPrice !== undefined) {
      const parsed = Number(minPrice);
      if (!Number.isNaN(parsed)) {
        whereClauses.push(`p.price >= $${paramIndex}`);
        params.push(parsed);
        paramIndex++;
      }
    }

    if (maxPrice !== undefined) {
      const parsed = Number(maxPrice);
      if (!Number.isNaN(parsed)) {
        whereClauses.push(`p.price <= $${paramIndex}`);
        params.push(parsed);
        paramIndex++;
      }
    }

    const stock = parseBoolean(inStock);
    if (stock !== undefined) {
      whereClauses.push(`p.in_stock = $${paramIndex}`);
      params.push(stock);
      paramIndex++;
    }

    if (rating !== undefined) {
      const parsed = Number(rating);
      if (!Number.isNaN(parsed)) {
        whereClauses.push(`p.rating >= $${paramIndex}`);
        params.push(parsed);
        paramIndex++;
      }
    }

    const allowedSortColumns = new Set(['created_at', 'price', 'rating', 'name']);
    const safeSortBy = allowedSortColumns.has(sortBy) ? sortBy : 'created_at';
    const safeSortOrder = sortOrder.toUpperCase() === 'ASC' ? 'ASC' : 'DESC';
    const safeLimit = Math.min(Math.max(Number(limit) || 20, 1), 100);
    const safeOffset = Math.max(Number(offset) || 0, 0);

    const sql = `
      SELECT
        p.id,
        p.name,
        p.category,
        p.price,
        p.in_stock,
        p.rating,
        p.created_at,
        p.updated_at
      FROM products p
      WHERE ${whereClauses.join(' AND ')}
      ORDER BY p.${safeSortBy} ${safeSortOrder}
      LIMIT ${safeLimit}
      OFFSET ${safeOffset}
    `;

    const products = await repository.query(sql, params);

    const countSql = `
      SELECT COUNT(*)::int AS total
      FROM products p
      WHERE ${whereClauses.join(' AND ')}
    `;

    const countResult = await repository.query(countSql, params);
    const total = countResult?.[0]?.total ?? 0;

    return res.json({
      data: products,
      pagination: {
        total,
        limit: safeLimit,
        offset: safeOffset,
      },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return res.status(500).json({
      message: 'Failed to search products',
      error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error',
    });
  }
});

export default app;
